title: IRT Growth Curves, Lag-CRP, and PLI Recency

answers.student_hint: IRT growth curves for LTP093: mean IRT (ms) per (total correct on the list, transition position) -- the points seaborn plots, one line per total-correct
between-subject IRT growth curves over the 24-session cohort (session 23 dropped): mean IRT (ms) per (total correct, transition position)
Lag-CRP for LTP093 session 0 only: conditional response probability at each lag (-23..23), NaN at lag 0
Lag-CRP for LTP093 averaged over all of the subject's ltpfr2 sessions
between-subject Lag-CRP over the 24-session cohort (per-subject CRP at each lag; seaborn averages across subjects)
PLI recency curve restricted to lists 10-24: per-subject proportion of prior-list intrusions coming from list_lag lists back (seaborn averages across subjects)
